compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
## Why
The single `state["signature"]` slot is last-writer-wins, so one kind clobbers another.
This is the root-cause fix and the riskiest slice (back-compat + compaction), done first.
It is ADDITIVE and behavior-preserving: it introduces the `attestations` map without
changing any existing signature/mirror semantics, so it is safe to ship before the gate
migration (929e).
## What
Add `state["attestations"]`, a kind-keyed map (per-key LWW, like `process_workflow_run`).
`process_signature`:
- MIRROR (unchanged semantics): `state["signature"] = record` for EVERY SIGNATURE event,
  EXACTLY as today — including a blank/retired event (manifest=[], retired=True), which
  still clears the mirror. This preserves 100% of current behavior for the four existing
  `state.get("signature")` consumers and for `retire_attested_pin` (which stays in this
  slice; it is removed only in 929e, together with the reopen-aware validity that replaces
  it — so there is NO window where reopen-invalidation is missing).
- MAP (new, additive): if the event is kindable (non-empty manifest with a derivable kind),
  also set `attestations[kind] = record`. Kind = SIGNED `manifest[0]` substring before the
  first ":" (authoritative); `data["kind"]` is used only as a hint iff it matches, else
  ignored. A blank/retired/unkindable event is SKIPPED for the map (it stays out of the
  map; it does not and cannot clear a kind). Nothing in this slice READS the map yet, so the
  map's presence is inert until cc1b/929e wire consumers to it.
Also add `state["last_reopened_at"]` (in `process_status`): the timestamp of the most recent
`closed -> open` transition (pre-update status == "closed" and target == "open"); null if
never. (Consumed by compute_validity in 929e; recorded here so the data exists.)
Read fold-in: an OLD SNAPSHOT whose compiled_state carries only `signature` (no
`attestations`) is folded into the map on restore, under the kind derived from that
record's own `manifest[0]` (dropped if blank/unkindable). New SNAPSHOTs carry BOTH
`attestations` and the `signature` mirror automatically (the reducer maintains both).
`_present.py` `public_state()`: add a DEDICATED `attestations` branch stripping the
`signature` (HMAC hex) subkey from each per-kind record (the flat `_INTERNAL_SUBKEYS`
cannot express a wildcard kind level); retain the legacy single-`signature` strip.
114f owns the canonical per-record shape.
## Scope
`src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` (`process_signature`, `process_status`,
`process_snapshot`), `src/rebar/reducer/_present.py`, `src/rebar/reducer/_state.py`,
`ticket_state.schema.json`. No change to the signed payload; no change to retirement /
mirror semantics; `retire_attested_pin` untouched here.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `attestations` map added; `process_signature` sets the mirror for EVERY event (old semantics preserved, incl. blank/retired clearing the mirror) AND additively sets `attestations[kind]` only for kindable events.
- [ ] Kind from authoritative `manifest[0]`; `data["kind"]` only a validated hint; blank/unkindable events skipped for the map (never clobber a kind).
- [ ] `state["last_reopened_at"]` records the most recent closed->open timestamp (null if never).
- [ ] Old-snapshot fold-in files the legacy record under its manifest-derived kind (dropped if blank/unkindable); new SNAPSHOTs carry both map and mirror.
- [ ] `public_state()` dedicated `attestations` branch strips HMAC hex from every per-kind record; legacy strip retained; no hex in show/MCP.
- [ ] Compaction/SNAPSHOT round-trips all kinds; empty `attestations` omitted; `retire_attested_pin`/mirror semantics unchanged in this slice.
compiled_state.title: Reducer + state model: attestations{} kind-keyed map
